data.narrative:
CarbonCapture Inc. is a Series B climate and energy technology company currently valued at $300M post-money. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of five same-stage, same-sector private companies (Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B, Pano AI at $500M, Sage Geosystems at $400M, Reverion at $310M, and Cemvita Factory at $120M) alongside one public comparable, supplementing limited market data in frontier energy.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-subsequent-stage step-ups, implies a valuation of $570M from the current $300M post-money base. Peer-median analysis across the five same-stage, same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $400M, with a range spanning $120M to $5.0B—reflecting material dispersion in the climate technology market.

These methods imply a valuation range of $340M (low) to $656M (high), with a median of $485M. The current $300M valuation sits below the derived median, suggesting modest upside on stage-typical progressions and peer benchmarking. Key uncertainties include the extreme width of peer comparables—driven by Commonwealth Fusion's $5.0B outlier status—and limited visibility into capital efficiency metrics across the peer set, which would refine stage-appropriate multiples for early-stage deep tech.
